CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-07-2026 Continued To Jul-15-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-26-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT

Appearances:
Alistair Blacklock of the Office of the City Attorney (415.554.3888, alistair.blacklock@sfcityatty.org) for defendant and moving party;
Jeffrey Bayer (628.877.9097, jeff@jeffbayer.com) plaintiff and opposing party appearing in propria persona.

Argued and the court takes the matter under submission. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-19-2026 Continued To Jun-03-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-27-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Appearances:
Jeffrey Bayer, in court, in propria persona Plaintiff.
Alistair Blacklock, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, Deputy City Attorney, (415) 554-3888, for Defendant.

At the outset, the court notes that Plaintiff has not properly notified the court and interested parties of the intent to appear this morning to argue the court's tentative ruling. The court advises that it will hear argument as both parties are present but reminds plaintiff of his obligation to follow court protocol re the preceding, moving forward. The matter is argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The City and County of San Francisco's demurrer is sustained with leave to amend.

The City contends the action is time-barred because plaintiff's claims arise out of an interaction with police that occurred years ago. The court grants the City's request for judicial notice but cannot conclude from other pleadings that they discuss the same incident. Plaintiff's complaint does not state the date of his injury. The court may only sustain a demurrer on limitations grounds where the defect appears on the face of the pleading.

The City contends that the demurrer is uncertain. The court disagrees: its factual allegations are specific. A complaint must be liberally construed with a view to substantial justice. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 452.) The court construes the complaint as pleading an intentional tort in violation of the Penal Code provisions specified in the complaint. If there are additional causes of action plaintiff wishes to plead, such as statutory causes of action, he has leave to plead them.

The City contends that plaintiff has not complied with the Government Code claims-presentation requirement. Plaintiff has leave to amend to plead either compliance or facts excusing his compliance. The City presents a declaration establishing that there is no public record of the filing of a timely government claim. This is a proper subject of judicial notice (see Gong v. City of Rosemead (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 363, 376), but plaintiff may plead contrary facts if he can in good faith or he may plead facts excusing compliance with the claims-presentation requirement.

If plaintiff wishes to amend, he shall do so by February 27, 2026.

A proposed order has been submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)

Motion For Consolidation Of Cases Cgc-22-600482 & Cgc-25-630667
Off Calendar Jan-27-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF JEFFREY BAYER's Motion For Consolidation Of Cases Cgc-22-600482 & Cgc-25-630667.

Appearances:
Jeffrey Bayer, in court, in propria persona Plaintiff.
Alistair Blacklock, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, Deputy City Attorney, (415) 554-3888, for Defendant.

At the outset, the court notes that Plaintiff has not properly notified the court and interested parties of the intent to appear this morning to argue the court's tentative ruling. The court advises that it will hear argument as both parties are present but reminds plaintiff of his obligation to follow court protocol re the preceding, moving forward. The matter is argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Jeffrey Bayer's motion to consolidate this matter with Bayer v. City & County of San Francisco, Case No. CGC-22-600482, is denied. Case number 600482 was removed to federal court, depriving this court of jurisdiction, and this court's docket does not reflect that it was remanded. Case number 600482 is therefore not pending in this court and the court may not revive its jurisdiction by consolidating the cases.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)
